tools.name: database_operations
tools.description: Perform operations on semantic models hosted in Power BI Desktop or Fabric service. Supported operations: Help, Create, Update, List, ImportFromTmdlFolder, ExportToTmdlFolder, ImportFromBimFile, ExportToBimFile, ExportTMDL (YAML-like format), ExportTMSL (JSON script format), DeployToFabric. Use the Operation parameter to specify which operation to perform.

connectionName.description: Uses last connection if omitted. For ImportFromTmdlFolder/ImportFromBimFile/Create, names the new offline connection (auto-generated if omitted)

operation.description: Help, List, Update, ImportFromTmdlFolder, ExportToTmdlFolder, ImportFromBimFile, ExportToBimFile, DeployToFabric, Create, ExportTMDL, ExportTMSL

databaseName.description: Optional for Update with active database

maxReturnCharacters.description: Maximum characters to return (-1=no limit, 0=don't return, >0=limit). Default: 10000

refreshType.description: Full, ClearValues, Calculate, DataOnly, Automatic, Add, Defragment. For Refresh only

maxReturnCharacters.description: Maximum characters to return (-1=no limit, 0=don't return, >0=limit). Default: 10000

help.description: Perform operations on Analysis Services databases

value.description:
List all databases on the server.
For offline connections, returns information about the single loaded database.
For online connections, lists all databases on the server.
Mandatory properties: None.
Optional: ConnectionName.

Update an existing database's modifiable properties.
Names cannot be changed - use existing database name only.
Supports updating Description, CompatibilityLevel, Collation, Language, and Annotations.
Mandatory properties: UpdateDefinition. The database name can come from UpdateDefinition.Name, DatabaseName, or the currently bound database on the connection.
Optional: DatabaseName (must match UpdateDefinition.Name if provided), CompatibilityLevel, Description, Collation, Language, Annotations.

Create a new empty database with an empty model.
Currently only supports offline database creation (IsOffline defaults to true).
Creates a new offline connection automatically if ConnectionName not provided or doesn't exist.
Mandatory properties: CreateDefinition (with Name).
Optional: IsOffline (defaults to true), Description, CompatibilityLevel, Collation, Language, Annotations, ModelName.

Create an offline connection from a TMDL folder.
Imports and deserializes a TMDL folder structure into a new offline database connection.
Automatically generates connection name if not provided.
Mandatory properties: TmdlFolderPath.
Optional: ConnectionName (auto-generated if not provided).

Serialize a database to a TMDL folder.
For online connections, TmdlFolderPath is mandatory.
For offline connections, uses stored folder path if available, otherwise TmdlFolderPath is mandatory.
Mandatory properties: TmdlFolderPath (for online connections or offline connections without stored path).
Optional: TmdlFolderPath (for offline connections with stored path).

Create an offline connection from a .bim (JSON-serialized TOM) file.
Imports and deserializes a .bim file into a new offline database connection.
Automatically generates connection name if not provided.
Mandatory properties: BimFilePath.
Optional: ConnectionName (auto-generated if not provided).

Serialize a database to a .bim (JSON-serialized TOM) file.
Exports the database from the current connection to a .bim file.
Mandatory properties: BimFilePath.
Optional: ConnectionName.

Export database to TMDL (YAML-like syntax) format.
TMDL is a human-readable, declarative format for semantic models.
For online connections, DatabaseName is mandatory.
For offline connections, exports the loaded database.
Mandatory properties: DatabaseName (for online connections).
Optional: DatabaseName (for offline connections), TmdlExportOptions.

Export database to TMSL (JSON syntax) script format with specified operation type.
TMSL generates executable JSON scripts for database operations.
For online connections, DatabaseName should be specified.
For offline connections, exports the loaded database.
Mandatory properties: TmslExportOptions (with TmslOperationType).
Optional: DatabaseName, RefreshType, IncludeRestricted, MaxReturnCharacters.

Deploy a database to Fabric using TMSL createOrReplace script.
Supports deployment via direct connection string or workspace name.
Requires either TargetConnectionString OR TargetWorkspaceName in the request.
Mandatory properties: DeployToFabricRequest (with either TargetConnectionString OR TargetWorkspaceName).
Optional: TargetTenantName (defaults to 'myorg'), NewDatabaseName, IncludeRestricted, ConnectTimeoutSeconds, ClearCredential (defaults to false).

Describe the tool and its operations.
Provides comprehensive information about all available database operations.
Mandatory properties: None.
Optional: None.

notes: For Update operation, supply the database name via UpdateDefinition, the DatabaseName field, or a connection already bound to a database.
For DeserializeFromFolder operation, the TMDL folder path must be provided.
For SerializeToFolder operation, the TMDL folder path must be provided.
For Deploy operation, the target connection name must be provided.
For CreateOffline operation, the CreateDefinition must be provided.
For ExportTMDL operation, the ConnectionName and DatabaseName must be provided.

tools.name: trace_operations
tools.description: Perform trace operations on semantic models to capture and analyze Analysis Services events. Supported operations: Help, Start, Stop, Pause, Resume, Clear, Get, List, Fetch, ExportJSON. Use the Operation parameter to specify which operation to perform.

operation.description: Help, Start, Stop, Pause, Resume, Clear, Get, List, Report, ExportJSON

events.description: For Start. Defaults: CommandBegin, CommandEnd, QueryBegin, QueryEnd, VertiPaqSEQueryBegin, VertiPaqSEQueryEnd, VertiPaqSEQueryCacheMatch, DirectQueryBegin, DirectQueryEnd, ExecutionMetrics, Error

filterCurrentSessionOnly.description: Default: true. False captures all sessions

columns.description: For Fetch. Default: EventClassName, EventSubclassName, StartTime. Available: EventClassName, EventSubclassName, TextData, DatabaseName, ActivityId, RequestId, SessionId, ApplicationName, CurrentTime, StartTime, Duration, CpuTime, EndTime, NTUserName, RequestProperties, RequestParameters, ObjectName, ObjectPath, ObjectReference, Spid, IntegerData, ProgressTotal, ObjectId, Error

help.description: Capture and analyze Analysis Services trace events for query execution monitoring, debugging, and performance analysis.
help.authentication: Uses existing connection established via connection_operations tool.
capabilities: Capture Analysis Services events (query, storage engine, DirectQuery, etc.)
Fetch captured event summaries (event names and start times)
Export full event details to JSON file

Start.description:
Start a trace on the specified connection.
Optional properties: ConnectionName, Events, FilterCurrentSessionOnly.
FilterCurrentSessionOnly (default: true):
- true: Filter events to only the current session
- false: Capture all events from all sessions
If Events is not specified, default events will be captured: CommandBegin, CommandEnd, QueryBegin, QueryEnd, VertiPaqSEQueryBegin, VertiPaqSEQueryEnd, VertiPaqSEQueryCacheMatch, DirectQueryBegin, DirectQueryEnd, ExecutionMetrics, Error.
Only one trace can be active per connection.
Cannot start trace on offline connections - requires active server connection.
Optional: ConnectionName, Events, FilterCurrentSessionOnly.
tips: If Events is not specified, default events will be captured
Default events: CommandBegin, CommandEnd, QueryBegin, QueryEnd, VertiPaqSEQueryBegin, VertiPaqSEQueryEnd, VertiPaqSEQueryCacheMatch, DirectQueryBegin, DirectQueryEnd, ExecutionMetrics, Error
Only one trace can be active per connection
Cannot start trace on offline connections - requires active server connection
FilterCurrentSessionOnly=true is recommended for most scenarios to reduce noise and improve performance
FilterCurrentSessionOnly=false should be used when you need to monitor all server activity
Session filtering uses SessionID, ApplicationName, SPID, and always captures ExecutionMetrics events

No need to call this operation explicitly to collect DAX query execution metrics for performance analysis. Call Execute operation on the dax_query_operations tool and set GetExecutionMetrics parameter to true. That operation will implicitly start trace if necessary.

Stop.description:
Stop active trace on a connection.
Returns summary of captured events.
All captured events are discarded.
Optional: ConnectionName.

Pause.description:
Pause event capture on active trace (events are discarded while paused).
Trace continues running but events are not captured.
Optional: ConnectionName.

Resume.description:
Resume event capture on paused trace.
Events will be captured again after resuming.
Optional: ConnectionName.

Clear.description:
Clear captured events without stopping trace.
Trace continues running and capturing new events.
Optional: ConnectionName.

Get.description:
Get trace details for a connection.
Returns trace name, status, duration, and event counts.
Does not include captured events in the list for performance.
Use Fetch operation to retrieve captured events.
Optional: ConnectionName.

List.description:
List all traces across all connections.
Returns trace information for all connections with traces.
Does not include captured events in the list for performance.
Use Fetch operation to retrieve captured events.
tips: Returns trace information for all connections with traces
Does not include captured events in the list for performance
Use Get operation to retrieve full trace details including events

Fetch.description:
Fetch captured trace events with specified columns.
By default, includes only EventClassName, EventSubclassName, and StartTime.
Use Columns parameter to specify which columns to include.
By default, retains events after fetching.
Set ClearAfterFetch=true to clear events after fetching.
Optional: ConnectionName, ClearAfterFetch, Columns.
tips: Default columns: EventClassName, EventSubclassName, StartTime

Available columns: EventClassName, EventSubclassName, TextData, DatabaseName, ActivityId, RequestId, SessionId, ApplicationName, CurrentTime, StartTime, Duration, CpuTime, EndTime, NTUserName, RequestProperties, RequestParameters, ObjectName, ObjectPath, ObjectReference, Spid, IntegerData, ProgressTotal, ObjectId, Error
Specify Columns as an array to customize the report output

ExportJSON.description:
Export captured trace events to a JSON file.
By default, retains events after export (ClearAfterFetch defaults to false).
Set ClearAfterFetch=true to clear events after export.
Required: FilePath.
Optional: ConnectionName, ClearAfterFetch.

Help.description:
Describe the trace operations tool and its operations.
Provides detailed information about all available operations.

notes: Only one trace can be created per connection at a time.
Traces are automatically filtered on the server side to the current session.

If Events parameter is not specified when starting a trace, default events will be captured: CommandBegin, CommandEnd, QueryBegin, QueryEnd, VertiPaqSEQueryBegin, VertiPaqSEQueryEnd, VertiPaqSEQueryCacheMatch, DirectQueryBegin, DirectQueryEnd, ExecutionMetrics, Error.
Paused traces discard new events but can be resumed.
Trace operations are not supported on offline connections.
All operations are read-only and do not modify the model.

tools.name: named_expression_operations
tools.description: Perform operations on semantic model named expressions and Power Query parameters. All operations support both single and multiple items via list properties. Supported operations: Help, Create, Update, Delete, Get, List, Rename, CreateParameter, UpdateParameter, ExportTMDL. For Create/Update/CreateParameter/UpdateParameter operations, provide Definitions. For Delete/Get/ExportTMDL operations, provide References. For Rename, provide RenameDefinitions. Batch options allow control over transaction behavior and error handling.

operation.description: Help, Create, Update, Delete, Get, List, Rename, CreateParameter, UpdateParameter, ExportTMDL

kind.description: M for PowerQuery, DAX for calculations

maxReturnCharacters.description: Maximum characters to return (-1=no limit, 0=don't return, >0=limit). Default: 10000

help.description: Perform operations on semantic model named expressions and Power Query parameters.

value.description:
Create one or more named expressions in the semantic model. Named expressions are M expressions that can be shared across multiple tables/partitions and enable scenarios like parameterized data sources, shared logic, and temporary overrides via OOL bindings during refresh operations.
Mandatory properties: Definitions (list of NamedExpressionDefinition objects with Name, Expression, Kind).
Optional: Description, LineageTag, SourceLineageTag, QueryGroupName, Annotations, ExtendedProperties, Options (ContinueOnError, UseTransaction).
commonMistakes: Using Create for Power Query parameters instead of CreateParameter operation
Missing Kind property (required for Create, auto-set by CreateParameter)
Incorrect parameter expression syntax when creating parameters manually
tips: Use CreateParameter for Power Query parameters - it handles metadata and syntax automatically
Use Create for generic M expressions like shared query logic or data transformations
Kind is always set to 'M' since named expressions are Power Query expressions

value.description:
Update one or more existing named expressions. Names cannot be changed; use the Rename operation instead.
Mandatory properties: Definitions (list of NamedExpressionDefinition objects with Name matching target expression).
Optional: Expression, Kind, Description, LineageTag, SourceLineageTag, QueryGroupName, Annotations, ExtendedProperties, Options (ContinueOnError, UseTransaction).
commonMistakes: Using Update for Power Query parameters instead of UpdateParameter operation
Attempting to change the Name property (use Rename operation instead)
Breaking parameter metadata when updating parameter expressions manually
tips: Use UpdateParameter for Power Query parameters - it preserves metadata and handles syntax
Use Update for generic M expressions like shared query logic or data transformations
Omit properties to keep current values, set to empty string to clear (except Expression which cannot be empty)

value.description:
Delete one or more named expressions from the semantic model.
Mandatory properties: References (list of NamedExpressionReference objects with Name).
Optional: Options (ContinueOnError, UseTransaction).

Retrieve detailed information about one or more specific named expressions.
Mandatory properties: References (list of NamedExpressionReference objects with Name).
Optional: Options (ContinueOnError).
tips: Use List operation first to see all available named expressions
Parameters will have metadata like IsParameterQuery=true in their Expression

value.description:
List all named expressions in the semantic model with basic information.
No mandatory properties required.
tips: Use this to discover available named expressions before Get, Update, or Delete operations
All named expressions have Kind='M' since they are Power Query expressions
Parameters are special M expressions with metadata - use CreateParameter/UpdateParameter for better handling

value.description:
Rename one or more named expressions by changing their identifiers.
Mandatory properties: RenameDefinitions (list of NamedExpressionRename objects with CurrentName and NewName).
Optional: Options (ContinueOnError, UseTransaction).
tips: Use this instead of Update operation when you need to change the name
Works for both generic named expressions and Power Query parameters
All references to the named expression will be automatically updated

value.description:
Create one or more Power Query parameters with proper metadata. Expression will be automatically converted to parameter format if needed.
Mandatory properties: Definitions (list of NamedExpressionDefinition objects with Name, Expression). Kind will be automatically set to 'M'.
Optional: Description, LineageTag, SourceLineageTag, QueryGroupName, Annotations, ExtendedProperties, Options (ContinueOnError, UseTransaction).
commonMistakes: Manually formatting parameter expression syntax (let the tool handle it)
tips: Preferred over Create for Power Query parameters - handles metadata automatically
Power Query parameters are also referred to as semantic model parameters or Power BI parameters
Provide simple values in Expression - tool converts to proper parameter format
Auto-adds metadata: IsParameterQuery=true, Type="Text", IsParameterQueryRequired=true
Example: Expression="MyDatabase" becomes '"MyDatabase" meta [IsParameterQuery=true, Type="Text", IsParameterQueryRequired=true]'

value.description:
Update one or more existing Power Query parameters. Expression will be automatically converted to parameter format if needed. Names cannot be changed; use the Rename operation instead.
Mandatory properties: Definitions (list of NamedExpressionDefinition objects with Name matching target parameter). Kind will be automatically set to 'M'.
Optional: Expression, Description, LineageTag, SourceLineageTag, QueryGroupName, Annotations, ExtendedProperties, Options (ContinueOnError, UseTransaction).
commonMistakes: Using Update operation instead of UpdateParameter for Power Query parameters
Manually formatting parameter expression syntax (let the tool handle it)
Breaking existing parameter metadata by using generic Update operation
tips: Preferred over Update for Power Query parameters - preserves metadata automatically
Provide simple values in Expression - tool converts to proper parameter format
Maintains existing parameter metadata while updating the value

value.description:
Export a named expression to TMDL format.
Mandatory properties: References (list with one NamedExpressionReference object containing Name).
Optional: TmdlExportOptions for customizing export format.

Retrieve detailed information about the named expression operations tool and its capabilities.
No mandatory properties required.

notes: Use the Operation parameter to specify which operation to perform.
Operations accept lists of items for bulk processing.
Single-item operations are represented as lists of one.
Use Options.ContinueOnError to control error handling in batch operations.
Use Options.UseTransaction to control transactional behavior.
Parameter operations create Power Query parameters with required metadata.
ExportTMDL exports a named expression to TMDL format.

tools.name: measure_operations
tools.description: Perform operations on semantic model measures. All operations support both single and multiple items via list properties. Supported operations: Help, Create, Update, Delete, Get, List, Rename, Move, ExportTMDL. For Create/Update use Definitions property. For Delete/Get/List/ExportTMDL use References property. For Rename use RenameDefinitions. For Move use MoveDefinitions. To move measures between tables, use 'Move' operation. Update operation cannot change table assignment - use Move instead. Batch options allow control over transaction behavior and error handling.

operation.description: Help, Create, Update, Delete, Get, List, Rename, Move, ExportTMDL

tableName.description: Optional if measure name is unique
Optional if measure name is unique

currentTableName.description: Optional if measure name is unique

maxReturnCharacters.description: Maximum characters to return (-1=no limit, 0=don't return, >0=limit). Default: 10000

help.description: Perform operations on semantic model measures.

value.description:
Create one or more measures.
Mandatory properties: Definitions (list of MeasureDefinition objects with Name, Expression, TableName).
Optional: Description, FormatString, IsHidden, IsSimpleMeasure, DisplayFolder, DataType, DataCategory, LineageTag, SourceLineageTag, KPI, DetailRowsExpression, FormatStringExpression, Annotations, ExtendedProperties, Options (ContinueOnError, UseTransaction).
commonMistakes: Forgetting to supply the host table of the measure to be created in Definitions[].TableName

value.description:
Update properties, except for name or table, of one or more existing measures.
Mandatory properties: Definitions (list of MeasureDefinition objects with Name, TableName).
Optional: Expression, Description, FormatString, IsHidden, IsSimpleMeasure, DisplayFolder, DataType, DataCategory, LineageTag, SourceLineageTag, KPI, DetailRowsExpression, FormatStringExpression, Annotations, ExtendedProperties, Options (ContinueOnError, UseTransaction).
commonMistakes: Cannot change tableName via Update - use Move operation instead

value.description:
Delete one or more measures.
Mandatory properties: References (list of MeasureReference objects with Name, TableName).
Optional: ShouldCascadeDelete (applies to all items, default: true), Options (ContinueOnError, UseTransaction).
tips: Use ShouldCascadeDelete to delete dependencies of the measures

value.description:
Get details of one or more measures.
Mandatory properties: References (list of MeasureReference objects with Name, TableName).
Optional: Options (ContinueOnError).

List all measures in specified tables, or all measures across tables if no filter specified.
Mandatory properties: None.
Optional: Filter (with TableNames and/or DisplayFolders arrays to filter results), MaxResults.

Rename one or more measures.
Mandatory properties: RenameDefinitions (list of MeasureRename objects with CurrentName, NewName, TableName).
Optional: Options (ContinueOnError, UseTransaction).

Move one or more measures to a different table.
Mandatory properties: MoveDefinitions (list of MeasureMove objects with Name, DestinationTableName, CurrentTableName).
Optional: Options (ContinueOnError, UseTransaction).
commonMistakes: Don't use Definitions[].TableName - use MoveDefinitions[].DestinationTableName
Don't use delete and recreate - Move operation handles the transfer
tips: Use this instead of Delete and Create for better performance

value.description:
Export measure to TMDL format.
Mandatory properties: References (single item with Name).
Optional: TmdlExportOptions.

Describe the tool and its operations.
Mandatory properties: None.
Optional: None.

notes: Use the Operation parameter to specify which operation to perform.
Operations accept lists of items for bulk processing.
Single-item operations are represented as lists of one.
Use Options.ContinueOnError to control error handling in batch operations.
Use Options.UseTransaction to control transactional behavior.
If the request is declined by the user, the operation should be aborted.

tools.name: object_translation_operations
tools.description: Perform operations on object translations within semantic model cultures. Supported operations: Help, Create, Update, Delete, Get, List. Use the Operation parameter to specify which operation to perform. Supported object types: Model, Table, Measure, Column, Hierarchy, Level, KPI.

operation.description: Help, Create, Update, Delete, Get, List

help.description: Perform operations on object translations within semantic model cultures.

value.description:
Create object translations (single or multiple).
Mandatory properties for each translation: CultureName, ObjectType, Property, Value, and object identification properties based on ObjectType.
Optional: CreateCultureIfNotExists (default: true), Options.UseTransaction (default: true), Options.ContinueOnError (default: false).
Object identification requirements by ObjectType:
- Model: ModelName
- Table: TableName  
- Measure/KPI: MeasureName (TableName optional)
- Column: TableName, ColumnName
- Hierarchy: TableName, HierarchyName
- Level: TableName, HierarchyName, LevelName

Update object translations (single or multiple).
Mandatory properties for each translation: CultureName, ObjectType, Property, Value, and object identification properties based on ObjectType.
Optional: Options.UseTransaction (default: true), Options.ContinueOnError (default: false).
Object identification requirements by ObjectType:
- Model: ModelName
- Table: TableName  
- Measure/KPI: MeasureName (TableName optional)
- Column: TableName, ColumnName
- Hierarchy: TableName, HierarchyName
- Level: TableName, HierarchyName, LevelName

Delete object translations (single or multiple).
Mandatory properties for each translation: CultureName, ObjectType, Property, and object identification properties based on ObjectType.
Optional: Options.UseTransaction (default: true), Options.ContinueOnError (default: false).
Object identification requirements by ObjectType:
- Model: ModelName
- Table: TableName  
- Measure/KPI: MeasureName (TableName optional)
- Column: TableName, ColumnName
- Hierarchy: TableName, HierarchyName
- Level: TableName, HierarchyName, LevelName

Get object translations (single or multiple).
Mandatory properties for each translation: CultureName, ObjectType, Property, and object identification properties based on ObjectType.
Optional: Options.ContinueOnError (default: false).
Object identification requirements by ObjectType:
- Model: ModelName
- Table: TableName  
- Measure/KPI: MeasureName (TableName optional)
- Column: TableName, ColumnName
- Hierarchy: TableName, HierarchyName
- Level: TableName, HierarchyName, LevelName

List all object translations, with optional filters.
Optional: ListFilters (with FilterCultureName, FilterObjectType, FilterObjectName, FilterProperty).

Describe the tool and its operations.
No mandatory properties required.

notes: Use Definitions for Create/Update operations (batch processing supported).
Use References for Delete/Get operations (batch processing supported).
Single-item operations are just lists with one element.
Use Options.UseTransaction (default: true) to control transaction behavior.
Use Options.ContinueOnError (default: false) to continue processing after failures.
For List operation, use optional filters (FilterCultureName, FilterObjectType, FilterObjectName).
Object identification uses specific properties instead of composite names.
Response includes Summary (TotalItems, SuccessCount, FailureCount, ExecutionTime) and Results array with per-item details.

tools.name: dax_query_operations
tools.description: Perform DAX query operations on semantic models. Supported operations: Help, Execute, Validate, ClearCache. Use the Operation parameter to specify which operation to perform.

operation.description: Help, Execute, Validate

executionMetricsOnly.description: When true with GetExecutionMetrics, omits row data but reads all rows for accurate timing. Default: false

help.description: Execute and validate DAX queries against semantic models.
help.authentication: Uses existing connection established via connection_operations tool.
capabilities: Execute DAX queries with configurable timeout and row limits
Validate DAX query syntax without execution
Capture detailed query execution metrics (storage engine, DirectQuery, cache metrics)
Clear database cache to force data refresh from source

Execute.description:
Execute a DAX query against a semantic model and return the results. 
Optionally capture detailed execution metrics including storage engine and DirectQuery metrics.
Mandatory properties: Query. 
Optional: ConnectionName, TimeoutSeconds, MaxRows, GetExecutionMetrics, ExecutionMetricsOnly.
tips: When GetExecutionMetrics is true, a trace will be automatically started if not already active and paused after metrics collection
If a trace is already active, it must have the required events subscribed for metrics collection
ExecutionMetricsOnly only takes effect when GetExecutionMetrics is true. When true, row data is not returned but all rows are read for accurate execution time

Validate.description:
Validate a DAX query for syntax and semantic correctness without executing it. 
Mandatory properties: Query. 
Optional: ConnectionName, TimeoutSeconds.

Help.description:
Describe the DAX query operations tool and its available operations. 
Mandatory properties: None. 
Optional: ConnectionName.

ClearCache.description:
Clears the cache for the specified database connection. This forces the semantic model to reload data from source.
Mandatory properties: None.
Optional: ConnectionName (uses last used connection if not provided).

notes: Query parameter is required for Execute and Validate operations.
TimeoutSeconds is optional (defaults: 200s for execute, 10s for validate).
MaxRows is optional for Execute operation to limit result size.
GetExecutionMetrics is optional for Execute operation to capture execution metrics (requires trace support).
ClearCache operation clears the cache for the entire database.

tools.name: perspective_operations
tools.description: Perform operations on semantic model perspectives, perspective tables, perspective columns, perspective measures, and perspective hierarchies. Supported operations: Help, List, Get, Create, Update, Delete, Rename, ListTables, GetTables, AddTables, UpdateTables, RemoveTables, ListColumns, GetColumns, AddColumns, RemoveColumns, ListMeasures, GetMeasures, AddMeasures, RemoveMeasures, ListHierarchies, GetHierarchies, AddHierarchies, RemoveHierarchies, ExportTMDL. Use the Operation parameter to specify which operation to perform. PerspectiveName is required for all operations except List.

operation.description: Help, List, Get, Create, Update, Delete, Rename, ListTables, GetTables, AddTables, UpdateTables, RemoveTables, ListColumns, GetColumns, AddColumns, RemoveColumns, ListMeasures, GetMeasures, AddMeasures, RemoveMeasures, ListHierarchies, GetHierarchies, AddHierarchies, RemoveHierarchies, ExportTMDL

perspectiveName.description: For AddTables, UpdateTables, RemoveTables, AddColumns, etc.

tables.description: For AddTables, UpdateTables, RemoveTables, GetTables. Remove/Get: only TableName required

columns.description: For AddColumns, RemoveColumns, GetColumns. Remove/Get: only TableName+ColumnName required

measures.description: For AddMeasures, RemoveMeasures, GetMeasures. Remove/Get: only TableName+MeasureName required

hierarchies.description: For AddHierarchies, RemoveHierarchies, GetHierarchies. Remove/Get: only TableName+HierarchyName required

maxReturnCharacters.description: Maximum characters to return (-1=no limit, 0=don't return, >0=limit). Default: 10000

help.description: Perform operations on semantic model perspectives, perspective tables, perspective columns, perspective measures, and perspective hierarchies.

value.description:
List all perspectives in the model with summary information. 
Mandatory properties: None. 
Optional: None.

Get detailed information about one or more perspectives including all tables, columns, measures, and hierarchies. 
Mandatory properties: References (list of PerspectiveReference objects with Name). 
Optional: Options (ContinueOnError).

List all tables included in a specific perspective with summary information. 
Mandatory properties: PerspectiveName. 
Optional: None.

Get detailed information about multiple tables in a perspective. 
Mandatory properties: PerspectiveName, Tables (list with TableName). 
Optional: None.

List all columns included in a specific perspective table. 
Mandatory properties: PerspectiveName, TableName. 
Optional: None.

Get detailed information about multiple columns in perspective tables. 
Mandatory properties: PerspectiveName, Columns (list with TableName and ColumnName). 
Optional: None.

List all measures included in a specific perspective table. 
Mandatory properties: PerspectiveName, TableName. 
Optional: None.

Get detailed information about multiple measures in perspective tables. 
Mandatory properties: PerspectiveName, Measures (list with TableName and MeasureName). 
Optional: None.

List all hierarchies included in a specific perspective table. 
Mandatory properties: PerspectiveName, TableName. 
Optional: None.

Get detailed information about multiple hierarchies in perspective tables. 
Mandatory properties: PerspectiveName, Hierarchies (list with TableName and HierarchyName). 
Optional: None.

Export perspective definition to TMDL (Tabular Model Definition Language) format. 
Mandatory properties: References (list with at least one PerspectiveReference containing Name). 
Optional: TmdlExportOptions.
Note: Only the first reference is used for export.

Describe the perspective_operations tool and its available operations. 
Mandatory properties: None. 
Optional: None.

notes: For CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE, and RENAME operations, the 'perspective_name' parameter is required.
For operations that require a table name, the 'table_name' parameter is required.
For operations that require a column name, the 'column_name' parameter is required.
For operations that require a measure name, the 'measure_name' parameter is required.
For operations that require a hierarchy name, the 'hierarchy_name' parameter is required.
For operations that require a new perspective name, the 'new_perspective_name' parameter is required.
For operations that require a new table name, the 'new_table_name' parameter is required.
For operations that require a new column name, the 'new_column_name' parameter is required.
For operations that require a new measure name, the 'new_measure_name' parameter is required.
For operations that require a new hierarchy name, the 'new_hierarchy_name' parameter is required.

tools.name: column_operations
tools.description: Perform operations on semantic model columns. All operations support both single and multiple items via list properties. Supported operations: Help, Create, Update, Delete, Get, List, Rename, ExportTMDL. Use the Operation parameter to specify which operation to perform. For Create/Update use Definitions, for Delete/Get/List/ExportTMDL use References, for Rename use RenameDefinitions. Batch options allow control over transaction behavior and error handling.

operation.description: Help, Create, Update, Delete, Get, List, Rename, ExportTMDL

maxReturnCharacters.description: Maximum characters to return (-1=no limit, 0=don't return, >0=limit). Default: 10000

help.description: Perform operations on semantic model columns.

value.description:
Create one or more columns. 
Mandatory properties: Definitions (list of ColumnDefinition objects with Name, TableName, and either Expression or SourceColumn). 
Optional: DataType, DataCategory, FormatString, SummarizeBy, DefaultLabel, DefaultImage, IsHidden, IsUnique, IsKey (DirectQuery only), IsNullable, DisplayFolder, SortByColumn, SourceProviderType, Description, IsAvailableInMDX, Alignment, TableDetailPosition, Annotations, ExtendedProperties, AlternateOf, GroupByColumns, Options (ContinueOnError, UseTransaction).

help.description: Perform operations on semantic model tables.

value.description:
Create one or more tables in the semantic model.
Mandatory properties: Definitions (list with Name, and one of DaxExpression, MExpression, EntityName, or SqlQuery for each).
- DaxExpression: For calculated tables
- MExpression: For M-script tables
- EntityName: For entity-based tables (requires either ExpressionSourceName or DataSourceName, optionally SchemaName)
- SqlQuery: For query-based tables (requires DataSourceName)
Optional: Description, DataCategory, IsHidden, ShowAsVariationsOnly, IsPrivate, AlternateSourcePrecedence, ExcludeFromModelRefresh, LineageTag, SourceLineageTag, SystemManaged, PartitionName, Mode, Annotations, ExtendedProperties.
IMPORTANT: For MExpression, SqlQuery, and EntityName tables, you MUST specify Columns with Name and DataType for each column - the schema cannot be auto-inferred from the expression. SourceColumn defaults to Name if not specified. For DaxExpression (calculated) tables, do NOT specify Columns as they are auto-derived from the DAX expression.
Batch options: Set Options.ContinueOnError to continue processing remaining items after failures (default: false). Set Options.UseTransaction for transactional behavior (default: true).
commonMistakes: Not providing Columns with Name and DataType for MExpression, SqlQuery, or EntityName tables
Not providing one of DaxExpression, MExpression, EntityName, or SqlQuery in Definitions
Not providing either ExpressionSourceName or DataSourceName when using EntityName
Not providing DataSourceName when using SqlQuery
tips: 'Measures' is a reserved word and cannot be used as a table name
DirectLake mode requires EntityName with ExpressionSourceName (and optionally SchemaName)
For single-item operations, provide a list with one element

value.description:
Update properties of one or more existing tables.
Mandatory properties: Definitions (list with Name for each).
Optional: Description, DataCategory, IsHidden, ShowAsVariationsOnly, IsPrivate, AlternateSourcePrecedence, ExcludeFromModelRefresh, LineageTag, SourceLineageTag, SystemManaged, Annotations, ExtendedProperties.
Batch options: Set Options.ContinueOnError to continue processing remaining items after failures (default: false). Set Options.UseTransaction for transactional behavior (default: true).

Delete one or more tables from the semantic model.
Mandatory properties: References (list with Name for each table to delete).
Optional: ShouldCascadeDelete (at request level, default: true) - when true, dependent objects (columns, relationships, etc.) will be automatically deleted.
Batch options: Set Options.ContinueOnError to continue processing remaining items after failures (default: false). Set Options.UseTransaction for transactional behavior (default: true).

Retrieve detailed information about one or more tables.
Mandatory properties: References (list with Name for each table to retrieve).
Returns table properties, columns, measures, hierarchies, and partition details.
Batch options: Set Options.ContinueOnError to continue processing remaining items after failures (default: false).

List all tables in the semantic model.
Mandatory properties: None.
Optional: None.
Returns summary information for all tables including column count, measure count, hierarchy count, and partition count.

Refresh data for a specific table synchronously using XMLA/TOM.
Blocks until the refresh completes. Best for quick recalculations.
For long-running data refreshes, use RefreshWithAPI instead.
This operation reloads data from the underlying data source(s).
Mandatory properties: References (single item with Name).
Optional: None.

Start an asynchronous data refresh for a table via the Power BI Enhanced Refresh REST API.
Returns immediately with a request ID. Use CheckStatusOfRefreshWithAPI to monitor progress.
Mandatory properties: References (single item with Name for the table to refresh).
Optional: RefreshType (Automatic, Full, DataOnly, Calculate).

Check the status of an async refresh started with RefreshWithAPI.
Mandatory properties: RequestId (from RefreshWithAPI response).

Cancel an in-progress async refresh started with RefreshWithAPI.
Only one refresh can run per dataset at a time — cancel is needed to start a new one.
Mandatory properties: RequestId (from RefreshWithAPI response).

Rename one or more tables and automatically update all references.
Mandatory properties: RenameDefinitions (list with CurrentName and NewName for each).
All DAX expressions, relationships, and other references will be automatically updated.
Batch options: Set Options.ContinueOnError to continue processing remaining items after failures (default: false). Set Options.UseTransaction for transactional behavior (default: true).

Retrieve the schema information for a specific table.
Mandatory properties: References (single item with Name).
Optional: None.
Returns detailed column definitions, data types, and relationships.

Export table definition to TMDL (YAML-like syntax) format for human-readable declarative model definition.
Mandatory properties: References (single item with Name).
Optional: TmdlExportOptions (with IncludeRestrictedInformation, IncludeRoleMembers).
TMDL is ideal for version control and collaborative development.

Export table to TMSL (JSON syntax) script format with specified operation type for generating executable scripts.
Mandatory properties: References (single item with Name), TmslExportOptions (with TmslOperationType).
Optional: RefreshType (for Refresh operations), IncludeRestricted (for Refresh operations).
TMSL generates JSON scripts that can be executed against Analysis Services.

Mark one or more tables as date tables in the semantic model.
Mandatory properties: MarkAsDateTableDefinitions (list with TableName for each).
Optional: DateColumnName — when omitted, auto-detection finds a suitable DateTime column based on uniqueness, key status, or relationship position.
Setting a table as a date table enables time intelligence functions (DATESYTD, TOTALYTD, SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR, etc.).
Batch options: Set Options.ContinueOnError to continue processing remaining items after failures (default: false). Set Options.UseTransaction for transactional behavior (default: true).
commonMistakes: Specifying a column that does not have DateTime data type
Not providing MarkAsDateTableDefinitions
tips: DateColumnName is optional — the engine will auto-detect a suitable DateTime column if one can be unambiguously identified
The date column should contain unique, contiguous date values covering the full range of dates in your data
Auto-detection looks for DateTime columns that are unique, are key columns, or are on the one-end of a relationship

help.description: Perform operations on semantic model relationships.

value.description:
List all relationships in the model.
Returns detailed information about each relationship including tables, columns, cardinality, and filtering behavior.
Mandatory properties: None.
Optional: None.

Create one or more relationships between tables.
In PowerBI relationships, the 'from' side is the many side (fact table in BI terms, child table in database terms),
and the 'to' side is the one side (dimension table in BI terms, parent/lookup table in database terms).
This follows standard foreign key relationship patterns where multiple records in the child table reference a single record in the parent table.
If no relationship name is provided, one will be auto-generated using the pattern FromTable_FromColumn_ToTable_ToColumn.
Mandatory properties: Definitions (list, each with FromTable, FromColumn, ToTable, ToColumn).
Optional: Name, IsActive, Type, CrossFilteringBehavior, FromCardinality, ToCardinality, SecurityFilteringBehavior, JoinOnDateBehavior, RelyOnReferentialIntegrity, Annotations, ExtendedProperties, Options.
commonMistakes: Setting the dimension table as 'from' side and fact table as 'to' side - should be the other way around
tips: PowerBI relationships are predominantly many-to-one
Occasionally one-to-one relationships are used, in which case CrossFilteringBehavior must be BothDirections
Many-to-many relationships are rarely used and should be created with caution
CrossFilteringBehavior values: OneDirection, BothDirections, Automatic
FromCardinality/ToCardinality values: One, Many
SecurityFilteringBehavior values: OneDirection, BothDirections
JoinOnDateBehavior values: DateAndTime, DatePartOnly
Type values: SingleColumn

value.description:
Update one or more existing relationships' properties.
Names cannot be changed and must use the Rename operation instead.
Mandatory properties: Definitions (list, each with Name).
Optional: IsActive, Type, CrossFilteringBehavior, FromCardinality, ToCardinality, SecurityFilteringBehavior, JoinOnDateBehavior, RelyOnReferentialIntegrity, Annotations, ExtendedProperties, Options.
tips: CrossFilteringBehavior values: OneDirection, BothDirections, Automatic
FromCardinality/ToCardinality values: One, Many
SecurityFilteringBehavior values: OneDirection, BothDirections
JoinOnDateBehavior values: DateAndTime, DatePartOnly
Type values: SingleColumn (currently only supported value)

value.description:
Delete one or more relationships from the model.
This operation permanently removes the relationships and cannot be undone.
Mandatory properties: References (list, each with Name).
Optional: Options.

Get detailed information about one or more specific relationships.
Returns comprehensive relationship properties including tables, columns, cardinality, filtering behavior, and current state.
Mandatory properties: References (list, each with Name).
Optional: Options.

Rename one or more relationships in the model.
Mandatory properties: RenameDefinitions (list, each with CurrentName and NewName).
Optional: Options.

Activate one or more relationships in the model.
Sets the relationship's IsActive property to true, enabling it for data filtering and calculations.
Mandatory properties: References (list, each with Name).
Optional: Options.

Deactivate one or more relationships in the model.
Sets the relationship's IsActive property to false, disabling it from data filtering and calculations.
Mandatory properties: References (list, each with Name).
Optional: Options.

Find all relationships connected to a specific table.
Returns list of relationships where the table appears as either the 'from' or 'to' side.
Mandatory properties: References (list with single item containing Name as table name).
Optional: None.

Export a relationship to TMDL format.
Mandatory properties: References (list with single item containing Name).
Optional: TmdlExportOptions (for controlling export formatting and scope).

Describe the relationship operations tool and its available operations.
Returns comprehensive help information including supported operations, examples, and usage notes.
Mandatory properties: None.
Optional: None.

notes: Relationship names are case-insensitive.
Relationship names must be unique within the model.
Relationship names must not contain spaces or special characters.

Get the model definition and properties including metadata, configuration settings, and structural information.

Optional: ModelName.

Create a new offline model within a new database. Only offline database creation is currently supported.

Optional: Name (defaults to current model), Description, StorageLocation, DefaultMode, DefaultDataView, Culture, Collation, DataAccessOptions, DefaultMeasureTable, DefaultMeasureName, DefaultPowerBIDataSourceVersion, ForceUniqueNames, DiscourageImplicitMeasures, DiscourageReportMeasures, DataSourceVariablesOverrideBehavior, DataSourceDefaultMaxConnections, SourceQueryCulture, MAttributes, DiscourageCompositeModels, AutomaticAggregationOptions, DirectLakeBehavior, ValueFilterBehavior, SelectionExpressionBehavior, Annotations, ExtendedProperties, BindingInfos.
Note: When ExtendedProperties, Annotations, or BindingInfos are provided, existing collections will be completely replaced (replace-all semantics).

Refresh the model to reload data from data sources, synchronously using XMLA/TOM.

For long-running data refreshes (Full, DataOnly), use RefreshWithAPI instead.

Each Reference requires: TableName, Name.

Create one or more partitions.

- Description, Mode, SourceType, QueryGroupName, Annotations, ExtendedProperties
- Source-specific: Expression, Query, DataSourceName, etc.
